joke:
Developer: Bhai code chal gaya!
Senior: Kaise?
Developer: Ye mat pucho, warna ruk jayega.

Girlfriend: Main ya game?
Boy: Pause karne de pehle, fir batata hoon.

Office me sabse dangerous line:
'Ek quick meeting rakhte hain.'
Semester ki padhai hamesha kal se shuru hoti hai.

Papa: Phone chhod de.
2 minute baad: Zara mera WhatsApp dekhna.

AI se assignment likhwa liya.
Teacher: Ab iska matlab bhi bata do.
Bas last match bolke 12 match aur khel liye.

Diet Monday se shuru hoti hai.
Pizza Sunday ko khatam hi nahi hota.

Story sirf unke liye daali thi.
Dekha sabne, reply kisi ne nahi kiya.

Salary aati nahi...
EMI pehle se gate pe khadi hoti hai.
Dost wahi jo tumhari beizzati pe sabse pehle hanse.
TV ke saamne baithke har Indian Virat Kohli se better advice deta hai.

Corporate me 'ASAP' ka matlab hota hai:
'Kal tak kar dena.'

Result se pehle sab Bhagwan ko yaad karte hain.
Result ke baad teachers ko.
Last seen hide karne wale hi sabse zyada online hote hain.

Gym membership li.
Ab bas uski EMI hi uth rahi hai.

Boss: Free ho?
Employee: Galti se 'Haan' bol diya.
Alarm aur meri dosti sirf snooze tak hi hai.

Assignment maine nahi likha.
Prompt ne likha.

Cart me 20 items.
Bank account me sirf confidence.

Teacher: Ye exam easy tha.
Class: Kiske liye?
Paise itni speed se jaate hain jitni speed se aate nahi.

Client: Bas ek chhota change hai.
Developer: RIP weekend.
Shaadi ke baad remote ka asli owner pata chalta hai.

'Sirf ek video' dekhne gaya tha.
2 ghante baad conspiracy theories dekh raha tha.
Coffee ke bina meri personality loading pe rehti hai.
Engineering ne hume degree se zyada patience diya.

Google Maps: 10 minute.
Traffic: Dekhte hain.
Weekend 2 din ka aur Monday 20 din ka lagta hai.
Real life boring ho to stories interesting bana do.

HR: Hum family hain.
Salary: Bas pocket money wali.
Question paper dekhte hi saare formulas strike pe chale gaye.
Pizza share karna aur password share karna dono mushkil hai.
Startup me salary kam aur motivation unlimited hota hai.

Dating app pe bio strong tha.
Conversation weak nikli.

Tech Support: Restart kiya?
User: Nahi.
Tech Support: Pehle wahi kar lo.
Guest ke jaate hi ghar ki breaking news shuru ho jaati hai.
UPI ka 'Payment Successful' dekhke hi sukoon milta hai.
Movie se zyada interesting interval ki snacks hoti hain.
'Seen' ka reply na aaye to dimaag khud web series bana leta hai.

Manager: Bas 2 minute ka kaam hai.
Employee: Matlab aaj ghar late hi jana hai.

Hostel ka khana aur attendance...
Dono se sabka emotional damage juda hota hai.

Chai peene ke baad lagta hai life sorted hai.
Bill dekhte hi reality aa jaati hai.
Monday itni jaldi aa jata hai jaise uske paas Fastag laga ho.

Squad: Last match.
Reality: Sunrise tak last hi chalta raha.

Intern ka kaam:
Coffee lana nahi...
Sabka asli kaam karna.

Trip pe photos 500.
Enjoy sirf 5 minute kiya.

Crush online aayi.
Confidence offline chala gaya.
Internet tabhi slow hota hai jab tumhe sach me jaldi hoti hai.
Biryani share karne wale log alag hi level ke mahaan hote hain.

Freelancer Sunday ko bhi kaam karta hai.
Bas office nahi jaata.

Papa ka calculator aur dimaag...
Dono GST se bhi tez chalte hain.
Enemy se kam, apni squad ki grenade se zyada darr lagta hai.

Fitness app roz motivate karti hai.
Main roz ignore karta hoon.

Facebook khola tha ek notification ke liye.
Purani memories dekhte-dekhte 1 ghanta nikal gaya.

Indian train aur mera patience...
Dono kabhi kabhi late ho jaate hain.
Mummy: Kuch nahi khaunga bolne ke baad bhi plate bhar deti hain.
Aim mera itna unique hai ki wall ko hi headshot lagta hai.
Diet tab tak successful rehti hai jab tak kisi ne samosa na dikha de.
Bank balance dekhte hi shopping ka mood automatically cancel ho jata hai.
GTA me traffic rules follow karne wale bhi legend hote hain.
Roommate ka charger hamesha 'hamara charger' ban jata hai.

Meeting ka summary:
Ye mail me bhi ho sakta tha.
Amazon cart me cheezein add karna free therapy jaisa lagta hai.
Sale shuru hote hi lagta hai sab kuch zaroori hai.
WiFi password yaad rakhna exam ke formulas se mushkil hai.
Battery 2% pe ho to har app unnecessary lagne lagta hai.
Adult banna matlab khud hi bill bharna aur khud hi rona.
Best friend wahi jo tumhari embarrassing photos delete na kare.
Itna lazy hoon ki motivation bhi khud chalke aaye tab shuru karunga.
Camera on hote hi sabki personality HD ho jaati hai.
Work From Home me office shirt aur pajama best combo hai.
Password itna strong rakha ki main khud login nahi kar pa raha.

Doctor: Stress mat lo.
Bill dekhte hi stress double ho gaya.

Engineer ka asli talent:
Deadline se pehle miracle kar dena.
Helmet ghar pe yaad aata hai jab traffic police saamne dikhe.

'Aap hamare liye important hain.'
Hold pe 20 minute baad bhi wahi feeling nahi aati.
Package out for delivery dekhte hi har awaaz pe gate khol deta hoon.

Cab driver: 2 minute.
Map: 12 minute.
Auto wale bhaiya ka meter sirf museum me hi dekha hai.

Developer: Yaar, mera code local pe bilkul mast chal raha hai!
Senior: Toh ek kaam kar, client ko tera computer hi gift kar dete hain.

Programming seekhne ke stages:
1. Print('Hello World') - I am GOD!
2. Syntax Error - Huh, easy fixing.
3. Logic Error - Hmm...
4. Seg Fault - *Starts looking for new career*
CSS seekhna aasan hai, bas subah 5 baje uth ke ek button ko center karne ki koshish karo, sab normal ho jayega.

Beta: Papa, log shaadi kyun karte hain?
Developer Papa: Kyunki jab team badhti hai, tabhi toh bugs (problems) handle hote hain!
Code likhte waqt 90% time sirf ye sochne me nikal jata hai ki is variable ka naam kya rakhu taaki doosra coder mujhe gaali na de.

Artificial Intelligence: Ek din main pure world pe control karunga.
Windows Update: Pehle mera system restart hone de, phir sochio.

Database Administrator: Mujhe ek naya ghar chahiye.
Agent: SQL format me chahiye ya NoSQL?
DBA: SQL! Partitioning to automatic honi chahiye.
Aache coders wo nahi hote jo code jaldi likhte hain. Aache coders wo hote hain jo stackoverflow pe bina post kiye solution dhoond lete hain.

Senior Coder: Ye kisne likha hai ganda code?
Junior: Maine, 6 mahine pehle sir.
Senior: Oh, actually structure bahut solid lag raha hai, smart logic!

Programmer's Diet:
BreakFast: Coffee + Bugs
Lunch: Cold Coffee + More Bugs
Dinner: Code Refactoring
Snacks: Tears and regret.

Python: Mujhe seekhna bahut aasan hai.
C++: Mujhe seekh ke dikhao toh maane.
HTML: Guyz, main bhi ek language hoon na? 🥺
Java/C++: Tum chup raho aur tag close karo.

Git commit messages over time:
1. 'Initial commit'
2. 'Added some features'
3. 'Fixing bugs'
4. 'Minor changes'
5. 'Pls work'
6. 'I hate my life'

Senior: Code test kar liya?
Junior: Haan sir, compile ho gaya, matlab chal hi jayega.
Compiler: *Laughs in runtime exception*
Baki sab thik hai par ye hardware engineers ko kya lagta hai ki software hardware ko automatically control kar lega bina drivers ke?

Internet Explorer: Guys, 2026 me hum viral ho rahe hain na?
Chrome: Bro, tumhara update kab ka band ho gaya hai.

Ek programmer ne doosre se poocha: Tumhaari girlfriend kaisi hai?
Doosra: Null pointer exception, access nahi kar paa raha.

Client: Mujhe simple app chahiye, bas WhatsApp jaisa interface ho aur Amazon jaisa checkout.
Developer: Budget?
Client: Rs 5000 + ek achi rating.
I fixed one bug today. My code celebrated by introducing three new ones. Teamwork makes the dream work.
People say money can't buy happiness. They've clearly never opened a fresh box of biryani.
"We need to talk" is the relationship version of 'Your system is about to restart.'
AI can write essays, generate art, and solve equations... but somehow my printer still refuses to cooperate.
Every meeting starts with 'Let's make this quick' and ends with everyone questioning their career choices.
My teammates always discover the enemy exactly one second before I discover the 'Defeat' screen.
Professors upload 400 slides and call it 'light reading.'

Amazon: 'Customers also bought this.'
Me: Stop exposing my impulse-control issues.
Every Indian becomes a professional cricket analyst the moment a wicket falls.
The hardest exercise at the gym is pretending you know how the machines work.

Me: Give me a simple answer.
ChatGPT: Here's a beautifully structured 17-point explanation.
Indian parents can hear a snack packet opening from three rooms away.
I opened Instagram for two minutes. Somehow it's tomorrow now.
Coffee doesn't solve problems. It just makes me energetic enough to create new ones.

Manager: 'Do you have five minutes?'
Translation: 'Cancel the rest of your afternoon.'
Half the vacation budget goes to food. The other half goes to airport coffee.
Password requirements are getting so complicated that one day they'll ask for my childhood nickname and blood type.
Nothing feels more luxurious than waking up naturally five minutes before the alarm.
Nothing builds confidence faster than deleting one line of code and watching everything somehow start working.
The only love triangle I enjoy is me, pizza, and extra cheese.

Boss: 'Take your time.'
Also Boss: 'Any updates?'... 37 seconds later.
I don't need movie reviews. If the popcorn is good, it's already a 10/10.
My study plan is always perfect... until the WiFi starts working.
That 'Payment Successful' sound heals emotional damage better than therapy.
Family groups have two speeds: silent for days... or 847 messages before breakfast.

I searched for one tutorial.
YouTube: 'Here's a documentary about penguins instead.'
Traffic lights are just suggestion boxes for some drivers.
The WiFi is always fastest when nobody actually needs it.

Real friends don't let you do stupid things...
They record them first.
Startup founders survive on three things: caffeine, optimism, and pretending sleep is optional.

Doctor: 'Avoid stress.'
My bills: 'Allow us to introduce ourselves.'
My squad's favorite strategy is landing together... and getting eliminated separately.

Every diet ends the same way:
'I'll start again on Monday.'

Teacher: 'There are no stupid questions.'
Student: 'Will this be on the exam?'
Teacher: '...Next question.'

Phone at 100%: Confidence.
Phone at 5%: Survival mode activated.
A best friend is someone who roasts you in public... then defends you in private.
99% of programming is convincing yourself the bug is impossible... until you find the missing semicolon.

Mom: 'There's food at home.'
Me: 'Can we order pizza?'
Mom: 'Read my first message again.'

Customer Support: 'Your call is important to us.'
My call: *Ages into a historical event.*

I don't miss my shots.
I just give the enemy a motivational warning.
I'm not lazy. I'm on energy-saving mode until further notice.

My bank balance and my motivation have one thing in common...
Both disappear before the month ends.

I don't have a shopping problem.
I have a 'Free Shipping Ends Tonight' problem.
Every meeting has that one person who says, 'I have a quick question...' and unlocks another 30 minutes.

Hostel roommates don't knock.
They just enter like they're paying your rent.

Tech Support's biggest achievement isn't fixing computers.
It's staying calm while asking, 'Is it plugged in?'
The TV remote changes hands faster than the batting order during a collapse.

Marriage is basically two people asking each other,
'What do you want to eat?' until both give up.

Influencers don't wake up.
They 'start their morning routine' with three cameras and perfect lighting.

People fear AI taking over.
I'm still waiting for autocorrect to stop embarrassing me.
Engineering teaches you that 'temporary fix' is just another way of saying 'future problem.'
Dad can detect an unnecessary light left on from a distance scientists still can't explain.

The fastest way to end an office conversation is to say,
'So... who's taking notes?'

I finally fixed the bug.
Now the app has developed a completely different personality.

Coffee isn't an addiction.
It's just my operating system.

AI writes better emails than me.
I still spend 20 minutes deciding whether to end with 'Thanks' or 'Best'.

A best friend is someone who says,
'This is a terrible idea.'
...while grabbing their keys.

The office printer has one life goal:
Break exactly five minutes before your deadline.

I don't rage quit.
I perform an unexpected emotional shutdown.
My attendance and my motivation have been avoiding each other all semester.

The recipe said 'serves four.'
Challenge accepted.

I posted one selfie.
Instagram immediately suggested I become an influencer.

Boss: 'We're all in the same boat.'
Pretty sure you're in first class.
Every living room has at least one uncle who could've captained the national team.

The family group has two settings:
'Good Morning' and political debates.

I paid for a gym membership.
My bank account got the workout instead.

Startups move fast.
Mostly toward another funding round.

Teacher: 'It's an open-book test.'
Me: Great.
Questions: 'Explain using your own understanding.'

Nobody has ever looked sad while holding hot pizza.
It's scientifically impossible.

My code has excellent security.
Not even I can get into it anymore.

"I'm fine."
The most expensive sentence you'll ever believe.

Amazon knows I don't need another gadget.
It just doesn't care.
Traffic jams are just group projects for cars.

I came for one tutorial.
I left knowing how ancient Romans made bread.

Sleep is free.
Which explains why my brain refuses to let me have it.
1% battery gives you superhero-level decision-making skills.

Dad's superpower is asking,
'Who left this light on?'
from another dimension.
Nothing tests your patience like waiting for 'Payment Successful.'
Hostel roommates treat your snacks like public property.
Movie snacks now cost enough to qualify as an investment.

Salary arrives like a celebrity.
Leaves like a magician.
My favorite debugging technique is staring at the screen until the bug gets uncomfortable and leaves.

I'm not procrastinating.
I'm giving tomorrow a chance to shine.

Programmers don't make mistakes.
We create undocumented features.

Friend: 'Can I have one bite?'
Me: 'You mean... one friendship-ending decision?'

Me: Give me a short answer.
ChatGPT: Here's a beautiful novel with bullet points.

Real friends don't judge your bad decisions.
They ask for updates.

Corporate life is just professionally saying,
'I have no idea what's happening either.'

I don't miss easy shots.
I just give the enemy false confidence.

Engineering teaches you one important lesson:
Google usually knows more than your textbook.

Tea isn't a drink.
It's a software update for Indians.

Instagram keeps asking what's on my mind.
Nice try, algorithm.

Manager: 'Any blockers?'
Me: 'Yes... Mondays.'

Doctor: 'Get more sleep.'
Netflix: 'Are you sure about that?'
The WiFi becomes strongest exactly when you decide to stop using it.

Relationships are mostly asking,
'Where do you want to eat?'
and rejecting every answer.

Customer Support:
'Your estimated wait time is 2 minutes.'
Calendar: 'Liar.'

The bug disappeared as soon as I started screen sharing.
Even it feared being judged.

Money talks.
Mine usually says,
'Goodbye.'

The syllabus always looks achievable...
until you open Chapter One.

One YouTube video later...
I'm somehow learning how to survive a shark attack.

Green light.
One car moves.
The rest spend two business days reacting.

People worry about AI taking jobs.
I'm still waiting for autocorrect to stop changing my name.

The office coffee isn't strong.
It just lowers your standards.

My crush smiled at me.
I'm still living off that memory.

I lifted one dumbbell.
Now I walk around like I own the place.

My cart has dreams.
My wallet has boundaries.

If coding burned calories,
I'd accidentally become a fitness influencer.
Dad's favorite hobby is reminding everyone how expensive electricity is.
Weekends should come with a pause button.

I wasn't losing.
I was collecting valuable life lessons.
Sharing pizza is proof that love has limits.

I replay conversations from three years ago.
My brain deserves an Oscar.

My code works.
I have no idea why.
I'm too scared to ask.

Coffee doesn't wake me up anymore.
It just reminds me I have responsibilities.
My favorite office feature is the 'Leave Meeting' button.

My squad says 'Stick together.'
Five seconds later we're playing five different games.
Indian parents can turn off every light in the house using only disappointment.
Debugging is just reading your own lies from last week.
That one person who replies 'OK' after reading a 12-line message deserves jail.

Movie tickets are expensive.
The popcorn is just there to finish the job.

I don't study under pressure.
I create the pressure first.
Checking my bank balance before shopping is called emotional preparation.

Me: Give me one sentence.
ChatGPT: Absolutely. Here's Chapter One.
The loudest cricket expert is always the one holding the TV remote.
"Where are you?" has started more investigations than the FBI.

I went to the gym today.
Mostly to refill my water bottle.
Tech Support deserves awards for pretending they've never heard the same problem 10,000 times.

Nothing is permanent.
Except that one temporary fix from 2019.
Instagram filters have more confidence than I do.

Teacher: 'This will only take five minutes.'
The clock: 'Good one.'

Friday night feels like freedom.
Sunday night feels like betrayal.

Pizza arrives.
Every diet plan leaves the chat.

AI can generate a thousand ideas.
I still can't decide what to eat.

The quickest way to end a meeting is to ask,
'Who's writing the minutes?'

I don't lose rank.
I donate points to the community.
Dad can smell the AC running from another floor.

If it compiled on the first try...
I've probably deleted something important.

One tutorial later...
I'm somehow watching a documentary about volcanoes.
The car behind me always believes I'm driving slower than physics allows.

I don't chase dreams.
I hit snooze and let them come back tomorrow.

"Your feedback is valuable to us."
Translation: 'We'll never read it.'

I don't trust people who say,
'I'm full.'
Dessert hasn't even arrived.

My code is like an onion.
Every layer makes someone cry.

You can borrow my charger.
You can borrow my hoodie.
You cannot borrow my biryani.

Boss: 'No pressure.'
Deadline: 'Allow me to introduce myself.'

The enemy team always plays like professionals.
My team plays like we're meeting for the first time.

College teaches many things.
Finding parking isn't one of them.

I opened Amazon to buy one cable.
Three hours later I almost owned an air fryer.

ChatGPT never judges my questions.
Unlike Google, which acts disappointed after the second typo.
The best sleep happens 10 minutes before the alarm.

Relationship status:
Waiting for someone who replies faster than customer support.

Traffic teaches patience.
Mostly because you have no other option.

I renamed one variable.
The entire application filed a complaint.
Indian parents can find the TV remote faster than Google can find search results.

Instagram: 'People you may know.'
Me: I barely know the people I already follow.

Every office has one person who's 'just asking a quick question.'
It's never quick.

Coffee before work.
Coffee during work.
Coffee to recover from work.

AI can write poetry.
I still can't write a decent email subject.

Half the cricket match is analysis.
The other half is everyone disagreeing with the analysis.

Teacher: 'Any questions?'
Class: *Silence*
Exam: 'Surprise!'

I fixed the production issue.
Now development has trust issues.
UPI has made carrying cash feel like downloading music on a CD.
Friends don't let friends make bad decisions alone.

Movie trailers have become so detailed,
I've already seen the ending.

I walked past the gym today.
That counts as cardio, right?

Writing code is easy.
Explaining it six months later is the hard part.

Typing...
Deleted.
The greatest plot twist of modern communication.

My bank account and I are in a long-distance relationship.
Mostly because the money keeps leaving.
YouTube recommendations know I'm procrastinating before I do.

Hostel rule #1:
If you bought snacks, you bought snacks for everyone.

The bug only appears when the client is watching.
It's shy the rest of the time.

I'm not late.
I'm just giving everyone else a head start.

My code has two modes:
Doesn't work.
Works... but nobody knows why.

The pizza arrived.
Suddenly everyone in the house remembered they were hungry.

Nothing spreads through an office faster than,
'Free food in the break room.'
My squad's strategy meeting lasts longer than we survive.

Engineering is turning,
'That should work'
into
'Why does this work?'

Amazon: 'Only 2 left in stock.'
Me: Nice try... okay, I'll buy it.

Every programmer has a folder named 'final'.
Mine also has 'final_final' and 'final_final_v2'.

Dad's GPS has only one instruction:
'Why didn't you take the other road?'

I skipped one ad.
YouTube took it personally.

Boss: 'Enjoy your weekend.'
*Schedules a meeting for Monday 8 AM.*
Every cricket fan becomes a selector after one bad over.

I removed one console.log.
Now I have no idea what's happening.

Coffee is cheaper than therapy.
That's all I'm saying.

The lane next to mine always moves faster.
Until I switch.

Couples don't argue about big things.
They argue about where to eat.

The code passed all the tests.
Production said, 'Not so fast.'

Instagram says,
'Be yourself.'
Then rewards everyone using the same filter.

I don't need more study time.
I need fewer distractions with WiFi.

There's no greater confidence than pushing code on Friday.
There's also no greater regret.

Movie villains explain their entire plan.
I'd leave halfway through the speech.

I joined the gym for six months.
My muscles are still reading the terms and conditions.

Code reviews are just professionally saying,
'Interesting choice... but no.'
The person who sends voice notes longer than two minutes has too much confidence.

Customer Support:
'Have you tried restarting it?'
Me: 'Have you tried believing in me?'

I wrote clean code today.
Tomorrow's me will still complain about it.
My bed has a stronger pull than Earth's gravity.

AI isn't replacing me.
It just finishes my homework before I panic.
The best friendships are built on sarcasm and stolen fries.
My favorite framework is whichever one I don't have to debug today.

Weekends are like software updates.
They're over before you notice anything changed.
